name: Torre del Águila Reservoir and Cañada Real Trail
name_original: Embalse De La Torre Del Aguila — circular pasando por la Torre del Águila
description: Hike a moderate 5.6-mile circular route around La Torre del Águila Reservoir, featuring a 14th-century watchtower and diverse scenery.
region_intro: Hiking around Utrera offers diverse landscapes for outdoor enthusiasts. The region features significant wetlands, including the Utrera Natural Reserve with its seasonal saltwater lagoons, and unique rocky formations known as "Torcalito." Trails traverse varied terrain, from Andalusian countryside and farmlands to areas with olive groves and reservoirs. This provides a range of options for different hiking preferences and fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Utrera The most popular hiking route is Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail, a 11.3 miles (18.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 48 minutes to complete. This route winds through scenic Andalusian landscapes, featuring tunnels and viaducts. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sierra de San Pablo y Montellano — circular from the population of Montellano, a moderate 6.5 miles (10.5 km) path. It offers expansive views with significant elevation gain. Local hikers also love the Torre del Águila Reservoir and Cañada Real Trail, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through diverse scenery around the Torre del Águila Reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes. Hiking around Utrera is defined by wetlands, impressive rocky formations, and scenic routes through countryside and olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Utrera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 130 hikers have used komoot to explore Utrera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Utrera?
A: There are over 25 hiking trails around Utrera, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 130 times, contributing to an average rating of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Utrera?
A: Utrera's hiking trails showcase diverse natural landscapes. You can explore significant wetlands, such as the Utrera Natural Reserve with its seasonal saltwater lagoons, and unique rocky formations often referred to as "Torcalito." Trails also traverse scenic Andalusian countryside, farmlands, and areas with olive groves and cereal crops.

Q: Are there any easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families in Utrera?
A: Yes, Utrera offers several easy hiking options. Approximately one-third of the trails are classified as easy, making them suitable for beginners or families looking for a relaxed outdoor experience. These paths often wind through gentle countryside or around natural features.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife, especially birds, on the trails around Utrera?
A: Yes, Utrera is an excellent area for wildlife spotting, particularly birdwatching. The Utrera Natural Reserve, with its shallow saltwater lagoons like Laguna de Zarracatín and Laguna Arjona, is crucial for avian fauna. During migration, you can observe numerous waterfowl, including flamingos, white-headed ducks, and purple gallinules, especially in winter or spring when the lakes are full.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Utrera?
A: Yes, there are several circular hiking routes available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009130864/sierra-de-san-pablo-y-montellano-circular-from-the-population-of-montellano" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de San Pablo y Montellano — circular from the population of Montellano&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive views, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009092247/torre-del-aguila-reservoir-and-canada-real-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre del Águila Reservoir and Cañada Real Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate circular route around the reservoir.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Utrera?
A: The best time to visit Utrera for hiking is generally during winter or spring. During these seasons, the wetlands are typically full, offering prime conditions for birdwatching, and the temperatures are more moderate for comfortable hiking. Summers can be very hot, making spring and autumn also good choices.

Q: What interesting landmarks or historical sites can I see near the hiking trails?
A: Utrera's surroundings are rich in historical landmarks. You can explore various castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2456715" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marchenilla Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334326" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Utrera&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2778669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hacienda de Gandul&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/385599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jesús Nazareno Bridge / Roman Bridge Alcalá&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010806207/via-serrana-utrera-and-los-molares-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Serrana: Utrera and Los Molares Loop&lt;/a&gt;, even pass by historical castles and churches.

Q: Are there any trails that feature impressive rock formations?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Utrera, locally known as "Torcalito," is famous for its impressive rocky landscape. This area features bizarre and fascinating rock formations, similar to Torcal de Antequera, which were shaped by millions of years of erosion. Hiking paths lead directly through these unique geological features, offering a moderate challenge.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Utrera?
A: The trails in Utrera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse scenery, from wetlands and rocky landscapes to peaceful countryside, and appreciate the variety of routes available for different fitness levels.

Q: Is there a trail that includes a reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009092247/torre-del-aguila-reservoir-and-canada-real-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre del Águila Reservoir and Cañada Real Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular route that leads through diverse scenery around the Torre del Águila Reservoir. This trail also features a 14th-century watchtower, adding a historical element to the natural beauty.

Q: Are there any long-distance trails or routes that are part of a larger network?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009104432/via-verde-de-la-sierra-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde de la Sierra Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a notable route that winds through scenic Andalusian landscapes. As a 'Vía Verde' (Greenway), it is part of a network of disused railway lines converted into cycling and walking paths, often featuring tunnels and viaducts.
tips: It is a watchtower, built in the 14th century, under the protection of Spanish Historical Heritage, currently in a state of ruin. It is located on a hill with wide visibility to the north of the Torre del Águila reservoir. It is possible to enter, it consists of two floors.

This swamp is dedicated to the irrigation of the region's agriculture. It has great landscape value despite its artificial nature. Passage and wintering place for a large number of waterfowl. One of the places most frequented by mountain biking fans.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Embalse De La Torre Del Aguila circular route?
faqs.answer: This specific circular route is approximately 9 kilometers (5.6 miles) long and typically takes around 2 hours and 27 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: Komoot rates this particular route as moderate. While many trails around the reservoir are considered easy, this specific circular path involves some gently sloping hills and covers a distance that requires a reasonable level of fitness.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the historic Torre del Águila, a 14th-century watchtower offering excellent views. You'll also enjoy expansive views of the La Torre del Águila Reservoir and the surrounding countryside. On clear days, you might even spot distant sierras.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Embalse De La Torre Del Aguila trail?
faqs.answer: The trail meanders through an area of small, gently sloping hills. You'll walk along the reservoir's shore, through areas with eucalyptus and tamarisk trees, and across pastureland. The path is generally well-defined, offering a mix of natural surfaces.

faqs.question: Is the Embalse De La Torre Del Aguila circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the area is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially around wildlife and other hikers, and to clean up after them. There are no specific restrictions mentioned for dogs on this trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn are often ideal due to milder temperatures. In summer, it can get quite hot, so an early start is recommended. Winter offers a chance to see more aquatic birds.

faqs.question: Are there any specific viewpoints or opportunities for wildlife spotting?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The Torre del Águila itself provides an excellent vantage point for panoramic views. The reservoir is also a notable spot for aquatic birds, especially during passage and wintering seasons, so keep an eye out for mallard ducks, spoon ducks, coots, and pochards.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details for this exact trailhead aren't provided, the area around the Embalse De La Torre Del Aguila generally offers parking facilities, often near picnic areas or access points to the reservoir. It's advisable to check local maps or Komoot's tour details for the most precise starting point and parking options.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there is no mention of any permits or entrance fees required to hike the Embalse De La Torre Del Aguila circular route. It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like water or restrooms along the route?
faqs.answer: While the area around the reservoir has facilities like barbecue areas and tables for picnicking, there are no specific mentions of water fountains or restrooms directly on the hiking trail itself. It's always recommended to carry enough water for your hike and plan for restroom breaks before or after your activity.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: For this moderate hike, we recommend bringing comfortable hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a camera for the scenic views and historical tower. Binoculars could also be great for birdwatching.
